1	"""cedarvisit — a tiny visit-log CLI.
2	
3	Commands: log (add a record) · list · total · filter <category> · top ·
4	budget <category> <amount> · report.
5	"""
6	
7	import argparse
8	import sys
9	
10	from cedarvisit import ops, store
11	
12	
13	def main(argv=None):
14	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="cedarvisit")
15	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
16	    add_p = sub.add_parser("log", help="add a visit record")
17	    add_p.add_argument("category")
18	    add_p.add_argument("duration", type=float)
19	    add_p.add_argument("--note", default="")
20	    sub.add_parser("list", help="list all records")
21	    sub.add_parser("total", help="sum the duration field")
22	    filter_p = sub.add_parser("filter", help="records in one category")
23	    filter_p.add_argument("category")
24	    top_p = sub.add_parser("top", help="highest-duration categories")
25	    top_p.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=3)
26	    budget_p = sub.add_parser("budget", help="set the budget for a category")
27	    budget_p.add_argument("category")
28	    budget_p.add_argument("amount", type=float)
29	    sub.add_parser("report", help="per-category count and total, plus overall")
30	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
31	
32	    records = store.load_records()
33	    if args.command == "log":
34	        ops.add_record(records, args.category, args.duration, args.note)
35	        store.save_records(records)
36	        print("recorded.")
37	    elif args.command == "list":
38	        for r in records:
39	            print(f"{r['category']}: {r['duration']} {r['note']}".rstrip())
40	    elif args.command == "total":
41	        print(ops.total_duration(records))
42	    elif args.command == "filter":
43	        for r in ops.filter_by_category(records, args.category):
44	            print(f"{r['category']}: {r['duration']}")
45	    elif args.command == "top":
46	        for category, subtotal in ops.top_categories(records, args.limit):
47	            print(f"{category}: {subtotal}")
48	    elif args.command == "budget":
49	        budgets = store.load_budgets()
50	        ops.set_budget(budgets, args.category, args.amount)
51	        store.save_budgets(budgets)
52	        print("budget set.")
53	    elif args.command == "report":
54	        if not records:
55	            print("no records.")
56	        else:
57	            budgets = store.load_budgets()
58	            for category, count, total in ops.category_report(records):
59	                line = f"{category}: {count} records, {total}"
60	                if ops.over_budget(budgets, category, total):
61	                    line += f" (over budget: {budgets[category]})"
62	                print(line)
63	            print(f"overall: {len(records)} records, {ops.total_duration(records)}")
64	    return 0
65	
66	
67	if __name__ == "__main__":
68	    sys.exit(main())
